prompt: When a client disconnects mid-response, Gin panics instead of handling the error gracefully. The panic originates in two places: render.JSON.Render calls panic(err) when the underlying WriteJSON fails (e.g. on a broken pipe), and context.Render also calls panic(err) when any renderer returns an error. These panics propagate uncaught and crash the goroutine. Replace the panic-based error handling with proper error propagation: render methods should return the error, and the context layer should push the error onto the context's error chain and abort the response rather than panicking.
human_annotation_notes: render/render.go defines the Render interface: `type Render interface { Render(http.ResponseWriter) error; WriteContentType(w http.ResponseWriter) }`. The interface contract specifies that Render returns an error — the method signature is the constraint. An agent that never reads render/render.go might implement the panic removal in render/json.go but miss that the Render interface guarantees error propagation is the intended pattern, or might implement it inconsistently with the interface contract. The context.Render method signature takes `r render.Render` — understanding the full interface (including WriteContentType) is needed to understand why Abort() is appropriate. render/data.go is noise from the CDM — the Data struct does not constrain the panic-removal fix.

assert_design.cpc_description: Solution must: (1) change render/json.go to return WriteJSON error directly without panicking, (2) change context.Render to use c.Error(err) to push the error, (3) call c.Abort() after the error, (4) not panic in any render code path

constraint_propagation_targets.description: render/json.go Render method must return the error from WriteJSON rather than panicking
context.Render must push the error to the context error chain
context.Render must abort the response after a render error
